name: Briefing Note: Scope & Research
description: Identifies the key issues, background context, stakeholders, and information gaps relevant to a briefing note topic.
prompt_template:
<role>
You are a senior Australian Public Service policy analyst with expertise in {{policy_area}}, working in {{agency}}.
</role>

<task>
Identify the key issues, considerations, and context relevant to a briefing note on the following topic.

Topic: {{topic}}
Audience: {{audience}}
Purpose: {{purpose}}
Target brief length: {{length}}
I'm also providing these reference documents to draw on:

<reference_documents>
{{key_documents}}
</reference_documents>

Produce a structured list covering:

1. **Key issues** – the main points that must be addressed in the brief (5–8 items, in priority order)
2. **Background context** – essential context the reader needs to understand the topic
3. **Stakeholders affected** – who has a stake in this topic and their likely positions
4. **Potential sensitivities** – political, legal, or operational risks the brief should acknowledge
5. **Information gaps** – what information you would ideally have but don't; mark these [VERIFY]

Be specific to the APS context. Use plain language. Where a claim requires verification, write [VERIFY: description].
</task>

variables.name: agency
examples: Department of Health and Aged Care

variables.name: topic
variables.description: The policy topic for the briefing note

variables.name: audience
variables.description: Who will read this briefing note

variables.name: length
variables.description: Target length of the briefing note — helps calibrate how much depth and detail to cover in the research

variables.name: key_documents
variables.description: Externally available documents only – eg published annual reports, public policy documents, ABS/AIHW statistics, publicly available evaluations. Do not paste OFFICIAL: Sensitive or classified material into AI tools not approved for that classification. If your agency permits it for the AI tool you're using, you can also reference or upload internal documents directly in the tool rather than pasting them here.
variables.placeholder: Paste excerpts from available reference documents here – eg relevant sections of an annual report, published evaluation, or public statistics...

beginner.notes: This is the first prompt in the workflow. Copy it, paste it into your AI tool, and start the conversation. You'll keep using that same conversation for all the steps that follow. Your job after this step is to review the issues list and remove anything that doesn't fit your context.

intermediate.notes: Review the issues list critically before moving to Step 2. Reorder priorities based on your knowledge of the audience. Add issues the model missed.

advanced.notes: Consider running this step multiple times from different perspectives (eg, policy, legal, financial) to build a richer issues list before structuring the brief.
advanced.prompt_modification: Add before </task>: 'Also identify: **Legal/regulatory dimensions** – relevant legislation, regulations, or court decisions; **Economic dimensions** – budget, cost, or economic impact considerations.'

known_limitations: The model's knowledge has a cutoff date – current events, recent policy changes, and the latest data will not be included.
Stakeholder positions and sensitivities are inferred from general knowledge, not your agency's specific intelligence.
Always review the issues list against your own knowledge before proceeding.
